symptoms.onset: this evening, after reading results online

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic surge is a core panic feature, time-locked to the trigger.
Palpitations are non-specific; warrant a brief ischaemic-features screen but do not point to ACS on their own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyperventilation / rapid breathing that cannot be slowed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperventilation drives the dizziness and derealization of a panic attack.

symptoms.symptom_name: Racing mind and derealization (subjective 'confusion') with preserved coherence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Derealization is a recognised panic symptom and, when orientation is preserved, argues for panic.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Fulminant hepatic failure with hepatic encephalopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Subjective 'confusion' must be distinguished from true encephalopathy by testing orientation and coherence; here it is derealization, not obtundation.
Interoceptive amplification of benign muscular tightness in a health-anxious patient.
RUQ discomfort mandates screening for the Charcot triad; benign tightness (not severe unremitting pain) argues against biliary pathology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptom onset time-locked to online research of the antibody result

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear psychological trigger with symptoms worsening on rumination is highly characteristic of panic/health anxiety.
Absence of fever/rigors makes biliary sepsis much less likely, though a single home reading is not definitive.

symptoms.symptom_name: No jaundice, dark urine or pale stools (patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Fulminant hepatic failure with hepatic encephalopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of jaundice/cholestatic features argues against acute hepatic decompensation, but self-report cannot exclude subtle icterus.
No jaundice reduces the likelihood of biliary obstruction/cholangitis.
Absence of pruritus is consistent with early, asymptomatic PBC rather than advanced cholestasis.
Absence of vomiting is consistent with the absence of acute biliary pathology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oriented and coherent — knows time, place and person (spouse-corroborated)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Fulminant hepatic failure with hepatic encephalopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved orientation and coherent conversation strongly argue against encephalopathy; this is the key discriminator obtainable via telehealth.
Derealization with intact orientation is typical of panic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pre-existing health anxiety with prior milder panic episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A background of health anxiety and prior panic raises the prior probability of a panic attack.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recently reported abnormal antimitochondrial-antibody result (patient's account)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: AMA positivity is the serological hallmark of PBC; it is the precipitant of the anxiety, not a cause of acute symptoms.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-tender abdomen with negative Murphy's sign and no hepatomegaly

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A benign abdomen with negative Murphy's argues strongly against acute biliary pathology — sealed clinician finding, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No asterixis (no flapping tremor) on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Fulminant hepatic failure with hepatic encephalopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of asterixis argues against encephalopathy — a clinician sign the AI Doctor cannot elicit remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Fulminant hepatic failure with hepatic encephalopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No scleral icterus argues against significant hyperbilirubinaemia — a clinician-confirmed finding, unreliable on video.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal / only slightly elevated ALP with structurally normal liver on recent ultrasound

investigation_details.investigation_name: Liver function tests and liver ultrasound (prior/EHR)
investigation_details.expected_result: ALP normal or only slightly elevated; structurally normal liver
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without labs/imaging the acuity cannot be confirmed; a telehealth service must rely on history and safety-netting.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Fulminant hepatic failure with hepatic encephalopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Near-normal synthetic/cholestatic markers and normal structure exclude acute decompensation — sealed lab/EHR data not available to the AI Doctor in real time.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Autonomic panic cluster with a clear psychological trigger
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Palpitations, hyperventilation and derealization time-locked to rumination in a health-anxious patient form a coherent panic-attack cluster.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Reassuring red-flag screen for the hepatic mimics
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Absence of acute hepatic decompensation / biliary sepsis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Afebrile, anicteric, coherent and not vomiting — the history-obtainable negatives that make the dangerous mimics very unlikely without excluding them to gold standard.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: GP liver function panel (bilirubin, ALP, ALT, AST, GGT, albumin, INR) — routine, via GP follow-up
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline and staging bloods for early PBC belong in routine GP/specialist follow-up, not emergency remote ordering; establishes synthetic function and cholestatic pattern.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Hepatology referral with fibroscan / consideration of biopsy for PBC staging
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Staging of AMA-positive PBC and decisions about ursodeoxycholic acid are specialist-led and non-urgent.
Ordering emergency investigations for a panic attack with a reassuring red-flag screen over-medicalises the presentation and reinforces health anxiety; reserve for emergent red flags.
Tachycardia is panic-driven; an ECG is not indicated remotely for uncomplicated panic and is reserved for GP follow-up only if ischaemic features (radiating/exertional chest pain) emerge.
